vitals.aid_stations: Self-supported; commercial services only; some staffed checkpoints

course_description.overview: Self-supported ultra-distance road cycling race starting and finishing in Utrecht, Netherlands. Mix of fixed routes and free routing between three checkpoints in neighboring countries: Germany’s Eifel region (CP1), Luxembourg valleys (CP2), and Belgium’s Ardennes with climbs like Stockeu and La Redoute (CP3). Final fixed route back to Utrecht.[2]

key_segments.name: CP1: Eifel region, Germany
key_segments.description: Hills surrounding the Ahr river; staffed checkpoint with short mandatory parcours.

key_segments.name: CP2: Luxembourg
key_segments.description: Smooth tarmac and plentiful switchbacks; staffed checkpoint.

key_segments.name: CP3: Belgium Ardennes
key_segments.description: Iconic climbs including Stockeu and La Redoute; staffed checkpoint.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demanding mix of strategic free routing and mandatory parcours through hilly terrain, valleys, and cobbles. Self-supported format requires full self-reliance within 100-hour limit.[1][2]

logistics.getting_there: Start and finish in Utrecht, Netherlands; accessible by train, bike, or car.
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported; use commercial services only (hotels, shops).
logistics.registration_details: Entry fee around €220; register via official website utrechtultra.com. Mass start format.[2][4]
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended (98-99% tarmac); carry all gear for self-support.
logistics.local_considerations: Routes cross France, Germany, Belgium, Luxembourg; plan for variable weather in May, border crossings seamless in Schengen area.

final_verdict.one_liner: Challenging self-supported road ultra blending free routing with iconic European climbs and cobbles.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-distance cyclists seeking adventure in Western Europe without full gravel demands.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those prepared for self-sufficiency and strategic navigation.
pro_tips: Prioritize lightweight bikepacking setup for 98% tarmac.
Study Vosges/ Ardennes cols and cobbles in training.
Plan resupplies around commercial services near checkpoints.
